reviews.review_description: I bought this yesterday, the ability to sit and look at these masterpieces in the quality and resoultion it provides is the main draw. The AI voices are not great, but the links to Wiki are a nice touch. If you are studying art, (don't reference the Wiki :) but being able to see the brush strokes, paint layers in undisturbed VR is unparraled. I am not an art fan, the radio is okay, but if you are on the quest 3, just open a browser and play the music in the background, which just adds another level of immersion. I would only ask the devs continue to add new art, and is possible ability to adjust the lighting of the environment for a more relaxing feel, Superb.

Ever since reading a random comment from someone who had seen Michelangelo's David in person, I've wanted to visit. But that seems highly unlikely to happen. This is the first VR app I've heard of that includes David, so I had to try. It was a tiny bit disappointing, which might not be the app's fault (I pictured him a lot taller). Still, better than nothing! I like all the other exhibits. I would LOVE a photography DLC (like Mapplethorpe, whose work I have seen in person and it left an incredible impression on me) or even Banksy (though I appreciate perhaps more modern work is harder to include due to copyright).  Another wishlist item would be the ability to "fly" so we could see taller art (like David) up close. (Oh, now I see the hover option in the menu. Wonderful!)

Revising my review:  I went back in and found that Many of the artworks do have pretty good resolution. When i first engaged the app, most of the art I looked at did not capture the details of the paintings as I had expected. The developers are responsive and seem committed to continued improvement of this app. It’s nice to be able to look at so many amazing pieces of art and read a little about each one. 


I am disappointed in the resolution of the paintings. It’s true that if you hang out awhile they get a bit clearer but not that clear. I have tried out other apps that toured art galleries and the resolution was incredible. But this one not so good. Mechanics are not great. It’s nice to have so many art pieces available for view but i can’t stand to look at them when they are not very clear /detailed. They appear almost like cartoon renderings.

developer_response.body: Thank you for giving ArtQuest another chance! I'll keep on working to upgrade lower-resolution works wherever better images are available. In the meantime, I really value feedback and suggestions as they help me improve ArtQuest, so please don't hesitate to reach out via email or on Discord.

reviews.review_description: I really like this app because you can get up close to the paintings and spend as long as you like examining them, with no crowds to fight. I like the way they are arranged in galleries and there are notes for most of the paintings. Didn't see a surrealist movement but there is plenty of other stuff, will take a long time to get through.

I am absolutely impressed with the app! Being able to admire the wonders that rest in museums from the comfort of your home is a priceless privilege! The amount of information is overwhelming, and the fact that I can get close to the artefacts, to zoom in until I notice the last brushstroke, without being scolded by any supervisor, seems to me a gain that cannot be found anywhere else. I appreciate carademono's direct communication with users, the immediate response to reasonable requests. The sculpture section adds a whole new universe to get lost in. I would have liked to have had such an app when I was younger, during my education period, or even at university when I attended art history courses! It is one of the apps that awakens your enthusiasm, and I, at the age of 54, feel young again, with a curious and open mind! In front of the beauty, my eyes open again! For me, it is a privileged place, full of beautiful aesthetics, where I forget about the stress of the day. My only regret: I don't have enough time to spend in this world! I will also try shared visits with friends in VR! I think that every school, especially those in disadvantaged areas or countries, should be equipped with some VR headsets, and this application should necessarily be offered for free, paid par the government. Just as well, a formula should be thought of that includes our elderly, in great need of beauty and communication! Thank you and congratulations, dear Carademono!

reviews.review_title: Viewing world renown art from your home
reviews.review_description: Really great app to view art in high detail and at true scale.  I live the ability to create my own galleries, but my favorite part is visiting the galleries created by my friends and seeing what caught their eye. There is so much art to view, I will probably never find it all on my own.

The quantity of masterpiece you have access here is mind blowing, stuff that I only find online in small quality, here’s high quality and life size right in front of you, I’ve seen 17 different rooms just with Leonardo’s work and I didn’t even see all of it. I’m very impressed with it.
￼

ArtQuest VR isn’t just a game—it’s a revolution in how we experience art. Imagine having the world’s greatest museums at your fingertips, without the crowds, queues, or "please don’t touch" signs. This is VR at its finest: a meticulously curated library of dozens of painters (with sculptors and more on the way!), organized by artist, museum, or movement. Filter chronologically or by popularity, then step right up to inspect every brushstroke—no velvet ropes required.
The attention to detail is staggering. Hover to zoom in on massive canvases, switch languages effortlessly, and lose yourself in the sheer joy of discovery. The studio’s frequent updates mean the collection is always growing, making this a living art encyclopedia.
But here’s the real magic: ArtQuest VR makes art accessible. Whether you’re a seasoned art lover or a curious newbie, this is the perfect way to explore movements, artists, and history—together. Highly recommend sharing a headset with non-gamers; it’s a game-changer for cultural education.

reviews.review_title: A celebration for art lovers old and new
reviews.review_description:
I confess, I am an absolute novice when it comes to art but have always wanted to learn.  That's why my eyes lit up with excitement when I happened upon this app.  It is nothing short of extraordinary!  Being able to have access to such a wide variety of art and see the paintings in their original scale is so appreciated especially with those who have limited means to travel to actual galleries. 

When someone loves an app as much as I do, I get greedy in wanting things that are almost perfect to be even better.  Here are some minor hopes and suggestions:

As vast as the library is, hope there will be ways to add even more artwork.  Have looked into some more obscure artists and some pieces are missing.  Not sure if this a built in limitation from the public domain art that this app draws from. That said there are endless artworks to last me for a long long time.

The graphic quality while amazing on most paintings, some have an odd effect.  Will look at the painting, then it says "loading" and all of a sudden the painting looks slightly blurry.  Can this be improved upon?
***Edit:  Heard from the developer that it turns out my internet connection resulted in some of the art not loading properly.  It is now working for me so this has nothing to do with the app, the quality is great!***

Finally, while I love the ability of creating your own custom collections, would love if the interphase was more detailed, allowing you to edit the labels of your galleries and perhaps an additional way to organize them other then order of popularity or chronology.  Maybe A-Z?

In conclusion, best art app far and away, in fact my favorite new app in meta that will provide endless hours of education, enlightenment and pure joy! Thank you, thank you, thank you!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your thoughtful review. I'm so glad you're enjoying ArtQuest!

If you're interested in adding obscure artists or some of their works, please drop by the Discord server (https://discord.com/invite/nMVcw48U9H) and let me know which. I can look into why they're not appearing and potentially add them.

I'd also love to know which specific paintings are getting blurrier -- that sounds like a bug that I need to look into.

Finally, I definitely have plans to implement more control over your favorites galleries. I'll get to work on that as soon as I finish adding sculpture, decorative arts, and archaeology galleries. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
This is such a great app !
As an art lover, being able to view so many great works in such detail without having to travel is a real joy !
Being able to get close to the works and see them in such detail without setting off an alarm of being warned by the attendants is such a relief. Also, not having the intrusive (but inevitable) barrier of protective glass allows study in a way that isn't possible with the original.
Well done and please keep adding features (sculpture ? installations ?)

To be honest, I never thought of myself as much of a fan of art. But after a recent trip to a museum, I had a little bit more curiosity than I used to when I saw this app available I took a chance on it. This is, no joke, one of the best apps available today of any kind. The ability to get basically nose length away from the Mona Lisa and see the actual brush strokes and cracks in the paint connects you with the art pieces in a very different way. I find myself so much more interested to learn about the different works of art and the different artists than I ever was before. This app is super well done, and the developer is constantly improving. They also have a very active discord channel.

We need apps of this quality and type to thrive in virtual reality so I really hope you decide to support this developer and purchase this app. I know you won’t regret it. It’s truly amazing.

Wow.  i'm absolutely blown away. i actually can't even believe this app is only $6. it seems like the app support for this is really great and i'm going to be spending countless hours in here.  while i'm sure it's really difficult to render high quality textured images.. this literally feels like you're looking at a real painting. you can really see the details and get very close. would be cool in the future to get even more of a hyperrender.. but it's still so impressive and entertaining and good enough to really feel like you are there. and easy five out of five. must have for people that appreciate the details of painting

reviews.review_title: A Grand Gateway to the World’s Great Art
reviews.review_description:
TLDR: Just get the app. Support this developer. The value is incredible for the price if you have even the slightest interest in art...

After a short time in ArtQuest VR, you genuinely start to feel like you're wandering through a museum filled with a lot of the greatest art in the world. You move from painting to painting and gallery to gallery, exploring artists, movements, and museums from across the globe.

What really makes the experience impressive is the scale. With over a million artworks drawn from more than 300 museums, there is an enormous amount to explore. You can search for a favorite artist and discover works you’ve never seen before, browse movements like Impressionism or Renaissance, or explore collections from museums you may never visit in person. How cool is that?

I’m not an art historian—just someone who loves museums and great art—and sometimes a painting can stop you in your tracks. ArtQuest VR lets you experience that from the comfort of your own home.

Unlike crowded museums, here you can take your time with a painting. Most works appear in high resolution, letting you study brush strokes and details up close. Updates have already improved image loading, and it’s also clear the developer is actively responding to user feedback and continuing to improve the experience.

You can create personal galleries, listen to narration about artists and movements, and even explore with friends in multiplayer.

If you enjoy museums, art history, or simply discovering beautiful things, this app is absolutely worth exploring.

You can tell a lot of thought and care went into building this experience.

For the price of a fancy cup of coffee, you get access to an incredible amount of art.

I recently stumbled upon this app by accident and, I must say, it was a very pleasant surprise. This app exemplifies what I consider be one of the most fantastic uses of VR. From the comfort of your chair, and for less than the admission price of a visit to even one museum, ￼you are able to travel the world and see virtually every artistic masterpiece available on the planet. The detail is obviously not as good as the original, but it is certainly quite well done and very high quality. Also, there is an audio description of each artwork that explains in detail what makes the piece so special.￼
Don’t even think about not purchasing this app! Get it! Then go and explore the finest masterpieces from the best artists in the world. ￼

What a wonderful app - access to so much art in high resolution, and it links to the wikipedia content on paintings and artists).  For anyone interested in art the app is a must-have (and a bargain!)The content is amazing and the developer is super-responsive. The UI and feature-set is still being expanded. Lots more to look forward to. Keep it up!

Traveling to see the world’s great art is expensive and it’s tough to get close to most paintings. This wonderful app lets you see so much wonderful art in a way you just can’t experience on a tablet or a book. I love it. Thank you for this app!

This app alone is worth having a meta quest headset. It's the biggest art collection you will find in VR, with a huge variety of styles and artists at your fingertips. Even if you are not an expert in art give it a go. You can learn a lot.

reviews.review_title: Been waiting for something like this.
reviews.review_description: This is the app I've been looking for for years.  So glad I stumbled upon this.  Tried it real quick the other day and immediately bought a copy for my sister.

I'm not really into art, but I appreciate the talent that goes into it.
Put some nice music on and explore all kinds of art. 
Maybe someday, you might do sculpture and ancient civilisations. Don't know if it's possible, 
though.

developer_response.body: I'm working on sculpture and ancient art / archaeology DLCs right now! They should be ready at some point in April.

reviews.review_description:
Absolute treasure trove. I have only owned for a few days and it is an amazing collection of art literally at my finger tips. Nothing else like this on quest.

My only wish now is for something similar for museums with artifacts.


Thoroughly reccomend

developer_response.body:
Sculpture and archaeological artifact DLCs are coming in early April! I'm putting the finishing touches on them now :)

Come drop by our Discord if you'd like a preview: https://discord.gg/nMVcw48U9H

reviews.review_description: I bought the APP and it's HUGE in art collections worldwide - Very clever to make a connection to Wikipedia for more details about the painting and the artist - perhaps in a future UPDATE to add some AI chatbot to ask Questions - very good price - it was 50% discount and I paid 5 euro - that's a cup of coffee in my country 😀

It opens up a world of art. Size it launched, they've made many improvements fixed, it's very actively maintained. I initially gave it five stars even several bugs and issues which are now fixed.

With this app you can explore many museum collections at once, see paintings in life size, step close and see the brushwork, and generally spend a lot of time with an artwork. And that's incredibly valuable. Because I don't know of any other way to experience paintings at their correct size and inspect them in such a natural way.

Looking at paintings on a computer can feel like Instagram, and museums are great but unless you're by yourself you've always got friends to keep up with, and usually I end up not having time to give a painting more than 120 seconds of my attention. Not to mention the security systems making it impossible to examine the paintings in detail.

Then there's the fact that for every painting you can see in a museum there's loads more in storage which you will not see today.  While with this app you can see many paintings currently in storage.

There are many clever technical details about this app - be sure to stay with a painting for a minute and let it load in full hd, some paintings are really amazingly high res. But the antialiasing is well implemented to minimize jagged and vibrating edges.

Just a super worth it experience.

Some possible additions: 
- Sometimes I wish I could elevate up and down with large paintings. 
- Custom galleries, like playlists in Spotify. (Update: They added this now!)
- Guessing games, like randomized galleries where your goal is to guess artist, date, genre etc ... Like In Google arts and culture.
- multiplayer of course so you can view paintings with your friends

reviews.review_title: Love the art just a couple of buts.
reviews.review_description:
The art looks amazing, but I , like some other reviews, can’t stomach the movement on the left controller - makes me feel sea sick. I have to shut my eyes when moving. 
I’d love to be able to search by genre - for instance ‘animals’, ‘the sea’ etc and also by painting name. I’m new to the app so apologies if I’ve just not found the correct controls etc. 
anyway, I’m pretty much housebound so this app has transported me to pictures I’ve seen before, but won’t again and pictures I’ll never see. 
Thank you.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review -- I'm really glad you're enjoying the art! To help with the motion sickness, try using the right controller for movement. The right thumbstick provides teleport controls and snap turning, which is much more comfortable than smooth motion. You can also point and click directly on a painting and it will teleport you in front of the painting. You should be able to use ArtQuest without any motion sickness at all if you only use the right controller.

reviews.review_title: I want to give this 5 stars but I cant..
reviews.review_description:
Let me just say there’s a lot to like about this app while also at the same time no….

I love the fact you can make your own custom gallery’s and I love the voice talking to you….

What I can’t stand? 

No volume button for the music, unnecessary slow loading times for absolutely no reason,
The lack of customization,  the fact the rooming just a contentious connection you don’t load into each gallery, also searching artist like Andy Warhol,   
Hating, Basquiat, dail, blanky or any of these people you think you should search you can’t…. It’s also extremely frustrating some of the art repeats

developer_response.body:
Thank you for trying out ArtQuest VR!

First some good news: I've already added a music volume control and it will be in the update coming next week -- along with multiplayer support. I'm also working on adding more customization options.

Unfortunately I can't legally include works by Warhol, Basquiat, Dali, etc because their artwork is not in the public domain. I have to comply with US and EU copyright law, and licensing their catalogs from their estates is a bit over budget for this app :)

Could you please help me understand the slow loading times and connection issues with gallery loading? I'm worried it might be a bug. I'd be grateful if you come by our Discord at https://discord.gg/nMVcw48U9H or send me an email at drcarademono@gmail.com.

reviews.review_description:
ArtQuest VR blew me away. The 15‑minute trial felt like seconds, and I bought it instantly — then bought it again on a second account. I’ve tried hundreds of Quest apps and nothing compares.

👍 HIGHLIGHTS
• Huge library of artworks — far more than I could ever see in a lifetime.
• Beautiful image quality and fast close‑up viewing.
• Chronological galleries that genuinely deepen understanding of each artist.
• Customisable frames, walls, and text.
• Smooth movement, simple controls, tiny download size.
• Emotional, immersive, and incredible value for the price.

👎 MINOR DOWNSIDES
• Occasional longer load screens.
• Limited voice options.
• Needs Wi‑Fi and navigation could be easier.

💡 WISHLIST
More voices, better sorting tools, and extra movements/artists (Bauhaus, Surrealism, Escher, Warhol, Mackintosh). A sculpture or architecture version would be amazing.

🎨 FINAL VERDICT
An astonishing app and an absolute bargain. If you enjoy art or galleries even a little, this is essential. It brings joy, emotion, and real wonder back to VR.

I've changed my rating from 1 to 4 star as the developer has been very busy with updates after many emails to resolve the issues.
The app is now working largely as intended and is an excellent platform for viewing a massive art collection. There is a delay when changing galleries but to be expected given the methods used to create them.
There is still room for improvement and the developer is very responsive in this respect.

This has to be the best art gallery app I have come across. The range of available artists and paintings from all time periods and across the globe due to the app design and online links are unrivalled and the quality on the Quest 3 is stunning. 

The gallery itself has some basic, but nice options to customise the environment, and the search and browse menu is easy to navigate. Movement around the gallery is good, with smooth motion or teleportation, both were intuitive enough that I didn't need to use the guide on the controllers. The direct links to Wikipedia were especially helpful for further information.

The only minor points that I found, was the first male voice was too slow, and I wasn't too keen on the one that was more natural speed. That's just personal preference though, and there were other voices to pick from. I also couldn't find a way of stopping the voice narration once I'd started it.
I'd also like to see a way to favourite certain artists, so I can easily pick from a personal favourites list to revisit them quickly, or perhaps a way to favourite paintings for a personal gallery too.

I haven't yet tried current artists in this yet, so I'm not sure if they're available as well, but there is so much to see that I could spend a lifetime just exploring.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the generous review! I'm thrilled you're enjoying ArtQuest so much!

To stop narration after it's started, you can click on the wall label a second time.

I would LOVE to have more contemporary artists, but currently the catalog is limited to artists whose works are in the public domain. So van Gogh, Monet, and Rembrandt are in, but not Picasso.

reviews.review_description:
This is now straight away one of my favorite apps on the Meta Quest. Some apps on the quest are more than just enjoyable, they justify owning and using a quest, and this is such an app. It's bringing the value of vr technology to a higher level. So first of all a great thanks to the developper for making this. 
Having said that, there is still work to be done to rate it 5 stars, although i can't hardly wait to do so. Most important issue for me, is having to stand very close to a painting before it downloads to full detail. I think the minimum that has to be done here is enlarging that distance, cause it's a bit frustrating needing to do it every time and kind of breaking the illusion. Ideally, i would prefer to give the user the option to fully download all paintings in each specific room before accessing it, maybe in combination with a download progressbar so you know the app hasn't stopped working completely. 
Another feature i would love to see added is the ability to move left and right in front of a painting exactly in the way you can now move up and down, that means slowly and exactly parallel to the painting, cause just walking left and right is going to fast and often also not parallel to the painting. The minimum speed of moving up and down I would prefer to see it even slower than now. 
If possible in the future, the museumhalls themselves could look more polished with some nice ceiling 'edges' etc. Also a subtle dynamic light reflection on the paintings could make it feel more realistic.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review and your feedback! I'm so glad you're enjoying ArtQuest VR.

I also find it frustrating to have to get very close to the painting to trigger the download, so I'll work on making it easier to trigger the download from a distance. There are some technical limitations preventing all the paintings from downloading at once -- many of the high-resolution images are quite weighty, and neither our servers nor your internet connection can easily handle 15 at a time! But I am planning on adding a higher-res thumbnail for large paintings so they don't look so blurry from a distance.

I'll look into adding sideways movement to the hover controls. I also have plans to add trim, base molding, better lighting, and other enhancements to the gallery rooms. But first I need to finish the two big new features I've been working on this past month: multiplayer support and galleries for sculpture, decorative arts, and archaeology.

reviews.review_description:
This app should be a must have for anybody with a minimum interest for arts, BUT I don't know why, the controlls seem to be adjusted for a different device. I show up like my eyes pwere on the ground level, and looking upwards make everything move upwards as well. I open the control panel and can't press any button, as my hands don't show up nor there are pointing lasers.
Prease provide some help and I will definitely give a 5 star.
UPDATE: just pressing oculus button while in the app seems to fix the issues. Crazy I got help from the discord right away. That was lovely. Now I need time to explore some galleries, but looks promising

developer_response.body: Come drop by the Discord server and we'll try to figure out what's happening with the controls: https://discord.gg/Rf2u8R67

reviews.review_description: I'm truly astounded by the quality of this app and I'm sure it will bring me much enjoyment. In case it helps anyone else - to begin with, I was just ending up in a small empty room without any paintings. I was just about to uninstall and reinstall on day 3, but everything was working perfectly on my latest attempt

Being able to comfortably enjoy high-resolution masterpieces in VR is one of the most effective applications of VR. This app allows you to enjoy the most masterpieces in super high resolution. In addition, the kind Wikipedia explanation helps a lot in appreciation.
Thanks for the multiplayer support that isn't common in this kind of app. The multi I tried with my family worked smoothly and was convenient, like auto-following. Using famous sculptures as avatars was also a fresh attempt. However, there are aspects where the pieces look a little gross. I think it would be okay if I could choose meta avatars or even simple ones.
Merci Lionel!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your generous review -- I'm so glad you're enjoying ArtQuest! I have to admit, I did try to get Meta Avatars to work but couldn't figure out how to do it. I might give it another try down the road, and I'll definitely work on improving the sculpture avatars and adding more options.

reviews.review_description:
This is GREAT for every art lover!!  Can spend hours here.
Issue:  Artists not listed:  Salvador Dali, Pablo Picasso, Jackson Pollock

Seems odd they shouldn't be there.  Picasso does have entries there, but he's not in the search.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review -- and I'm so glad you're enjoying ArtQuest VR!

I would love to have Dalí, Picasso, and Pollock in the app but unfortunately their work is still covered by copyright laws. Spanish copyright law is particularly strict, so  Dalí and Picasso's work won't enter public domain until the 2050s/60s! But I'll definitely put them in ArtQuest then :)

reviews.review_description: ArtQuest VR lets you wander through hundreds of real-world museum collections, viewing masterpieces at true scale with high-resolution detail and informative labels. It’s a relaxing cultural journey ideal for art lovers and curious explorers alike. Great app!

This is a beautifully done, a great experience that feels more like stepping into your own gallery. The controls are intuitive, but I would like the ability to sort the artists by date and by name. 
When you move to the optimum postion for the painting you do get to see it in quite good resolution, ideal for those that are not able to see go and see the painting in real life.  And the painting information is also of great use and very well done.  I do love the app and I look forward to the devs perhaps adding more mordern day artist like L.S. Lowry and others to the gallery, overall it is a relaxing and rewarding experience.
This reminds me so much of EarthQuest. It’d be awesome if it adds 'host and join' multiplayer in the future. It's basically the BRINK Traveler of the art scene!

developer_response.body: I'm so glad you're enjoying ArtQuest! Multiplayer support is under active development and I hope to have it ready sometime in February :)

reviews.review_description:
ArtQuest VR is an absolute Quest Store highlight: Thousands of masterpieces from Louvre, MoMA, Rijksmuseum & more in original size, with ultra-sharp zoom on brushstrokes and fascinating info – feels like a real museum visit, but better, no lines!

Super intuitive, runs smooth, and totally addictive. Hands down the best VR art experience!

Dev Team: Keep developing – you're killing it! Multiplayer mode (stroll galleries with friends & chat) would be epic and make it legendary.
Buy it now – a true gem! 🎨✨

developer_response.body: Thank you for the encouragement and support! I’m really glad you’re enjoying ArtQuest VR. I absolutely plan to add multiplayer support down the line -- strolling galleries with friends would be terrific.

reviews.review_description:
Great painting collection. Only a bit dizzy when you walk through the rooms. (a bit long loading time) Love the remarks by the paintings. Only miss modern art  for example from Banksy, Warhall and modern art. 
Hope the art is expanding. Like the idea for try-before-you-buy concept.

developer_response.body: I'm glad you're enjoying ArtQuest VR! I'd love to have more modern art too, but paintings by Banksy, Warhol, and others are still under copyright. For now the collection is limited to public domain work from 1931 and before. But I'm hoping to find a way to add exhibitions of modern art in the future!

reviews.review_description:
Updated review based on the developer’s kind reply: 

A very nice app that I would recommend to anyone interested in art. 
The developer even constantly add new features. Great support!
Give it a try, you won’t regret it!

This is must-get app in meta Quest.
Thank you for making such a great app.

I just discovered this app during the spring sale. I can't believe how much content this app gives you access to. The presentation is really well done and reading the other comments and Discord, it's clear the developer is listening and building something great here. As it is, it's worth checking out... with the enhancements the Dev is working on, this will be one of the few must have apps for the Quest. Well done !
It took me 2 minutes using the free demo before deciding to buy it, no regrets, anyone who loves art and art meuseums will enjoy the app
This is a gift from God. Profound.

reviews.review_title: Searching for this for a long time
reviews.review_description: I have been waiting for an app like this for a long time.   I wanted to have works by my favourite artists that I can study. Also, I like having them organized by the different galleries from around the world

As an artist and art lover, this VR app is a dream come true. The immersive experience, quality of photos, and inclusion of biographies and painting descriptions are exceptional. The paintings are true to scale (the most immersive aspect) since it’s difficult to grasp the artist’s work and intention in books or on screens. Having been to many galleries around the world, I can say this is one step closer to the real deal. Except it’s all in one place! Even more impressive is that this app offers not only a private retrospective of famous artists, but also of obscure ones. Easily 5 out of 5 stars, the best art history app by far. Big thanks to the developer for bringing this to life.
Enfin !!

reviews.review_title: Exactly What I Was Looking For
reviews.review_description:
Great but simple concept and a nearly endless amount of content. Higher resolution images load as you get closer and in many cases you can see incredible detail and brush strokes. The gallery itself could look a bit less sterile but I like that there are at least some customization options (frame material, floor material etc).

Edit: The issue below was fixed incredibly quickly and is no longer a problem, thank you! 

Dev, please look into likely bugged Quest 3 right hand controls. Turning and teleport movement seem to lock up consistently after a short time; I see the in game joystick/buttons react but I am unable to rotate!

I have already spent hours on it and just scratched the surface, bravo! 2 things for the developers, 1) sometimes going between galleries I end up outside and not obvious how to enter, 2) I wish there was a way to move up and down for viewing large paintings.

developer_response.body: I'm so glad you're enjoying ArtQuest! A way to move up and down is a good idea -- I'll add some hovering controls and do my best to track down the bug that's causing you to end up outside galleries.

reviews.review_description: This app has huge potential. It has a huge amount of art and information. I really like seeing the scale of the art and as an artist I really like studying the brush stokes up close. The developer is very quick to listen to concerns of the app and I’m positive it will get even better.

reviews.review_title: I've been waiting for an app like this
reviews.review_description: This is fantastic - really enjoy the groupings by artist or movement or collection. The information provided and ability to have it read to you is wonderful. Will recommend to anyone who, like me, has been hoping for something just like this!

reviews.review_title: Dream come true for browsing art
reviews.review_description:
As a native Chinese speaker, I was very happy to see that this application supports Chinese display. Although the basic information of the artworks lists both Traditional Chinese and Simplified Chinese at the same time, this is not a major issue. However, since Traditional Chinese can already be displayed, I hope the “Chinese” option in the menu can be separated into Traditional Chinese and Simplified Chinese, showing only the corresponding language.

If Chinese TTS narration for explaining the artworks could also be added, this would truly become a perfect artwork guide application.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review and your feedback! I'll do my very best to improve Chinese language support in the next update. I don't think I can add Chinese TTL yet because wit.ai (the Quest's TTL provider) doesn't currently support Chinese, but I'm pretty confident I can get Simplified and Traditional Chinese separated.

reviews.review_description: A must-have for art lovers! This app creates an immersive museum experience, with excellent quality images and descriptions. It’s easy to navigate and logically arranged. Perfect app for when you feel like wandering through an art museum and letting your mind explore great works of art.
I have been waiting for an app like this! I get to see great works of art, from my living room, it's great.
